---
name: ADR or principle change
about: Propose changing how the project thinks — a new ADR, an ADR supersession, or a change to PRINCIPLES.md.
title: 'ADR proposal: '
labels: ['adr']
---

<!--
Use this when you want to change the project's stated stance — not for code, bugs, or feature additions.

Per CONTRIBUTING.md, code never deviates from a principle in a PR. The flow is:
  1. This issue — describe the proposed change in stance.
  2. Public discussion. Consensus, or a maintainer authoritative call.
  3. A maintainer drafts an ADR PR capturing the new direction.
  4. The ADR merges; the new direction is now the project's stance.
  5. Any motivating code lands afterward, against the now-current stance.

Fill in what's relevant. The HTML-comment blocks below are for you, not for display — keep, edit, or delete them as you go.
-->

## What you're proposing

<!-- One or two sentences. New ADR? Supersession? PRINCIPLES.md change? -->

## Why

<!-- What forces are at play. What does the current stance cost. What outcome are you trying to enable. Which existing ADRs or sections of PRINCIPLES.md are in scope. -->

## Alternatives considered

<!-- Other ways to get the same outcome without changing project stance. Why aren't they sufficient. -->
